primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup monetary items (dividend receivables) as hedged items for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedge accounting to external party transactions in consolidated statements, while paragraph 6.3.6 provides the exception for intragroup monetary items when FX movements are not eliminated on consolidation under IAS 21. Sections 6.5.8-6.5.14 govern the hedge accounting requirements.

supporting_authority.reason: Determines whether foreign exchange gains or losses on intragroup monetary items are eliminated on consolidation, which is the condition for the paragraph 6.3.6 exception to apply.
Clarifies the nature of hedged risks and the scope of the hedge accounting exception for intragroup items, including when the foreign currency exposure of intragroup monetary items may qualify.

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses accounting for dividend distributions but does not govern hedge accounting treatment for foreign exchange exposure on dividend receivables.
Provides classification guidance for hedging instrument income/expenses but does not address eligibility of intragroup items as hedged items.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items under the IFRS 9 exception

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items when conditions are met
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6, foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation per IAS 21, such as when subsidiaries have different functional currencies.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup items in consolidated statements when conditions are not met
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5, hedge accounting applies only to transactions with external parties in consolidated statements; the intragroup exception in paragraph 6.3.6 is unavailable when FX movements on the intragroup monetary item are fully eliminated on consolidation.
